x.text:
Most meal-planning apps fail busy parents in one way:

They assume you have time to cook.

We built around 15-min dinners your kids will actually eat.

What's the one meal your family never says no to?

linkedin.text:
We studied 200 families to learn why meal-planning apps get abandoned by week 3.

The answer wasn't recipes. It was time.

Every competitor optimized for variety. Parents don't want variety on a Tuesday at 6pm — they want a 15-minute dinner that doesn't start a fight.

So we rebuilt the whole experience around three constraints: under 15 minutes, 5 ingredients, kid-approved.

Early result: 3x higher week-4 retention.

If you're building for busy parents, optimize for their hardest moment, not their ideal one.

What constraint does your product design around?
